If we miss this fundraising goal and our target finish in New Hampshire, I don’t believe we can continue contending at the same level. It’s that simple.

We’ve been driven to this situation by many problems. But the core is that our politics is broken and completely owned by the establishment and special interests.

Two billionaires are waging an ad war. A senior advisor this week on another campaign tweeted out instructions to a super PAC supporting his candidate. Is this democracy? Is this what the Democratic Party should be all about?

I don’t think so. That’s why I got in this race instead of sitting back and just being disgusted. And I know it’s why you’re here too. To stop making every single thing about money and special interests, and instead bring back some humanity to our economy and our politics.

That said, I also have to look at the reality of this race and what all indicators point to is that without a big jump in fundraising and support in the next few days, it is extremely challenging for a non-establishment, outsider candidate like me to compete.

**[I wish this was not the case, but I do believe New Hampshire is our last chance to break out. So, I’m asking you to give like you never have before. Contribute $10 or more now. ](https://act.yang2020.com/go/24808?t=12&akid=7178%2E377263%2E6b5A3u)**

Counting on you,

-Andrew
**We don't have corporate PACs or big donors who can close the fundraising gap we face, just grassroots donors like you powering this campaign. Please give now.** | [Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/24808?t=13&akid=7178%2E377263%2E6b5A3u)

name: Corky Messner
subject: Welcome to our first Corky for Senate newsletter!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
These weekly updates will include news from the campaign trail, spotlight our messaging and our mission, and provide you with information on upcoming events
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/62a394cb3cb5c973c3d6b838967d376c/33153) [ Donate Now! ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/674881e4d881dabbc3d6b838967d376c/33153) [ Contact Us ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/9222addab4966b38c3d6b838967d376c/33153) | |
---
Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative.
|
---
Welcome to our first _Corky for Senate_ newsletter! These weekly updates will include news from the campaign trail, spotlight our messaging and our mission, and provide you with information on upcoming events and how YOU can help Corky defeat career politician Jeanne Shaheen and bring common sense and conservatism back to the United States Senate.

We can do it, New Hampshire!
|
---
**IN THE NEWS**
The National Review spotlighted Corky’s efforts to win the U.S. Senate seat, pointing to his “sensible conservative agenda” and providing an overview of how he came to the decision to run against Jeanne Shaheen. As Corky notes: “Fundamentally, my principles are fiscal responsibility, limited government, accountability, and a strong defense. And to preserve for everybody, my kids, your kids, everybody in New Hampshire, the opportunity to live the American Dream.”

Read more [here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/953539b8fd59bd2bc3d6b838967d376c/33153).
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/4d95b3970e148f45c3d6b838967d376c/33153)
|
---
**QUICK PIC**
Corky was honored to meet Second Lady Karen Pence at a Nashua event on Feb. 6th. Despite the chilly, icy weather, the crowd was warm and enthusiastic!
|
---
**CAMPAIGN NOTES**
New Hampshire GOP stalwart Wayne MacDonald has been named campaign chairman. MacDonald previously served as New Hampshire Republican Party chair from 2011-2013 and held the position for several months in 2006. He also chaired former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie’s presidential campaign in the Granite State in 2015-16. Says Corky: “Wayne’s experience and commitment to advancing Republican values make him an invaluable ally as we work to bring common sense and conservative values back to the U.S. Senate.”

Also new to the campaign team is longtime Republican consultant Michael Biundo, a partner in the firm RightVoter LLC, who will serve as senior adviser and communications consultant. “Mike's experience nationally, and in New Hampshire, will be invaluable as our campaign ramps up after the first-in-the-nation presidential primary,” Corky asserted. “I am so happy to have him on our team, and specifically, being able to utilize his talents in this communications role.”
|
---
**CORKY'S QUOTE: PRESIDENT'S ACQUITTAL**
“I'm proud to stand by President Trump's win today. Despite this, NH's own Senator Shaheen voted to remove him. We need real representation to support the President. Help me retire Jeanne Shaheen!”
|
---
**UPCOMING TOWN HALLS**
Don’t miss our next town hall meetings with Corky! Meet the candidate and ask questions about the important issues facing Granite Staters.
* **PLYMOUTH** – Saturday, February 8, 4:30-6 p.m., Foster’s Boiler Room, 231 Main Street
* **HOOKSETT** \- Thursday, February 13, 6:30-8 p.m., Granite Tapas & Cocktail Lounge, 1461 Hooksett Road

Voters interested in attending can register via email at [info@corkyforsenate.com](mailto:info@corkyforsenate.com), visit the website at [www.corkyforsenate.com](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/159e1a88dda4d724c3d6b838967d376c/33153), or call the Messner campaign at 603-369-6109.
|
---
**MESSNER MINUTE**
**Democrats are Regressive, Not Progressive**
In the latest installment of our _Messner Minute_ video series, Corky explains how Democrats may claim to be progressive, but their stands on the issues and their pro-government, Big Brother policies are in fact regressive. And he references the US Constitution in making his case against those progressive/regressive policies.

[Watch the video](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/fa353f7f6446c97ec3d6b838967d376c/33153) and check out previous _Messner Minutes_!

[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/5fa4d25a837c7fd7c3d6b838967d376c/33153)
|
---
Corky Messner for Senate
[Email us](mailto:info@corkyforsenate.com)
[Visit our website](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/dc21b017d82aa45ec3d6b838967d376c/33153)

| [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/3bc8ee2a8beebbd5c3d6b838967d376c/33153) | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/5aea9f4fee699092/6b71d216295ade74c3d6b838967d376c/33153)
---|---
|

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Is this the end of the last best hope?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Powered By People

When the Constitution was adopted on June 21, 1788, it immediately became
the exception to the rule of human history, announcing to the world that
we would be governed by laws and not by men. To guard against human
tendency to concentrate power and authority — to keep would-be kings,
dictators, strongmen and thugs from wresting control of this noble
experiment — a system of checks and balances was implemented. There would
be separate branches of government, and a division of power and
responsibility between them. And in the sole branch of government where
power is not shared but held by one person, the executive, there would be
a further check: impeachment.

Though imperfect, and even immoral in its initial acceptance of slavery
and the slave trade, the Constitution was a breathtaking departure from
the course of human events and enabled America to become a singular,
inspiring idea.

Under the Constitution, disagreement is adjudicated without violence and
power is transferred peacefully. And though never fully realized, within
it there is enshrined the aspiration that we will all be treated equally
under the law and that each of us will have equal opportunity to guide the
affairs of this country through free and fair democratic elections.

Looking at so much of the rest of the world and almost the whole of human
history, it is clear that without the Constitution we would have suffered
the rule of men and not enjoyed something approaching the equal protection
and promise of just laws.

Today, two hundred and thirty-three years after its adoption, it has been
replaced by something else.

"Functionally a monarch," is how Presidential historian Jon Meacham
described Donald Trump earlier this week, after a feckless Senate majority
refused to call witnesses, subpoena documents, or hold anything remotely
resembling a fair trial after his impeachment by the House.

Some Republican senators — like Marco Rubio and Lamar Alexander — even
conceded that what the President did in extorting a foreign power for his
own political benefit was wrong and impeachable.

They just weren’t going to do anything about it.

Republican Senator Lisa Murkowski told reporters that "Congress had
failed"... as though she is a passive spectator of Congress instead of a
majority Senator who in fact possesses extraordinary leverage and power to
keep it from failing.

Perhaps she should have looked to Mitt Romney, her only colleague in the
majority to vote to convict Donald Trump — becoming the first and only
Senator in our history to vote to convict a President from his own party.

Romney explained that "the Constitution is at the foundation of our
Republic’s success, and we strive not to lose sight of our promise to
defend it."

At its core the President’s trial — our country’s trial — comes down to
this question: will we have the rule of men or the rule of law? Those
Senators who accepted the argument that the President can do anything he
chooses as long as it helps his re-election, as his defense team argued,
support the former. Those who choose to hold him accountable because of,
in Mitt Romney’s words, "an appalling abuse of the public trust," support
the latter.

By a 52–48 vote, our country, through our representatives in the Senate,
chose the rule of men.

Only some men, to be sure. Those powerful enough, shameless enough,
enabled enough, to live above the law.

This past Saturday I saw a photo of white men in camouflage, tactical gear
and face masks, armed with military-style assault weapons in the Capitol
Building in Frankfort, Kentucky. It was chilling to witness this
defilement of democracy by armed thugs who sought to intimidate lawmakers
and the citizens they represent.

Getty Images News [1]Verified @GettyImagesNews
[2]@GettyImagesNews Gun rights activists carrying semi-automatic
firearms walk through the Capitol Building on
January 31, 2020 in Frankfort, Kentucky.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42268?t=1001&akid=78930%2E444211%2ER6KaaE ]Gun rights activists carrying firearms in
Kentucky Capitol #1[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42268?t=1002&akid=78930%2E444211%2ER6KaaE ]Gun rights activists
carrying firearms in Kentucky Capitol #2

It wasn’t an aberration. Two weeks ago, similarly heavily armed and masked
men paraded through the Virginia capitol in an effort to intimidate
lawmakers who had the audacity to introduce bills requiring background
checks on firearms purchases. And it wasn’t unconnected to the impunity
with which the President acts or the breakdown of the rule of law in our
country.

Donald J. Trump [5]Verified @realDonaldTrump
[6]@realDonaldTrump [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42269?t=1003&akid=78930%2E444211%2ER6KaaE ]Trump's tweet

Those men marching up the capitol steps in Kentucky are listening to
Donald Trump. They anticipate a tide that is turning, and in the obscenity
of their act of masked intimidation presage the beginning of rule by men
and not by laws. Just like the white nationalist thugs who marched in
Charlottesville in 2017. Or the man who drove 600 miles from Allen, Texas
to El Paso, Texas last August, intent upon repelling an "invasion of
Hispanics." A man inspired by Trump, incited by Trump, and acting on the
hateful, violent, dehumanizing rhetoric employed by Trump. A man who
killed 22 people — children, women and men — people who wrongly believed
that the law and the institutions of this country would protect them.

This is now where violence waits, in the shadows and increasingly out in
the open, watching this hideous farce in the Senate... waiting for the next
signal from Donald Trump, knowing now that any remnant of restraint is
fast breaking loose.

We are watching in real time the destruction of the most noble experiment
in the whole of human history, a descent, as Adam Schiff put it, into
"constitutional madness." And if we are unable to stop this slide, the
victims will number more than just our democracy, our Constitution and the
institutions that have produced the most successful country in the history
of the world — they will include any of us not able to purchase power or
arm themselves with it.

This was the end of Adam Schiff’s closing argument to the Senate on
Monday:

I put my faith in the optimism of the founders. You should too. They
gave us the tools to do the job, a remedy as powerful as the evil it was
meant to constrain: impeachment. They meant it to be used rarely but
they put it in the Constitution for a reason:

For a man who would sell out his country for a political favor. For a
man who would threaten the integrity of our elections. For a man who
would invite foreign interference in our affairs. For a man who would
undermine our national security and that of our allies.

For a man like Donald J. Trump.

They gave you a remedy and they meant for you to use it. They gave you
an oath and they meant for you to observe it. We have proven Donald
Trump guilty, now do impartial justice and convict him.

Despite the outcome in the Senate and this darkest of moments for our
country, my faith is still in the optimism of the founders. And it is in
the people of El Paso who demonstrated that what happened here in August
was not the victory of violence and lawlessness, but instead the triumph
of a community that has stood resilient in resisting racism perpetrated at
the highest levels of power, a city that has demonstrated extraordinary
strength, compassion and kindness in the face of a welling evil in this
country.

We are tempted to despair, and that is understandable. But we must
understand that should despair take hold it will leave us defenseless
against the greatest challenge America has yet faced.

Perhaps the most alarming thing that came out of Iowa this week was not
the incompetence of the party and the failed technology that leaves us
still in the dark as to the final results of the caucus... what should most
concern us is that turnout might have barely kept pace with 2016 levels,
and fell well below the historic turnout of 2008. We’re in the middle of a
national emergency, and people are staying home.

Discussing this with a friend, he reminded me of how despair spread across
Germany in the 1930s — of all the people who were not Nazis, but neither
were they simply passive observers of the Nazis of all that transpired...
the countless thousands who were completely devastated by what they saw
happening to their country, felt utterly powerless to stop it, and, over
time, quietly retreated from the world into darkness and despair. And
today? How many millions are so heartbroken by what has come to pass in
America that they have already retreated from the world?

I refuse to be one of them. I still have faith in this country, faith that
we can follow the optimism of our founders, faith that we can follow the
example of El Paso... but it will take all we have from all of us who are
willing to fight to save this country to make it so. And in this struggle,
as Lincoln said at another defining moment in our history, "we shall nobly
save, or meanly lose, the last best hope of earth."

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=78930%2E444211%2ER6KaaE

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA

name: Ben Salango
subject: Team , we need a plan - not more talk.
body:
West Virginia deserves better than this.
Team,
This is an emergency. **The opioid epidemic is one of the biggest threats West Virginians face - and I want to tell you about my plan to fight it.**
[**Sign our petition to support a comprehensive, long-term solution to the opioid epidemic.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/14758360/155042819/-2062289957?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODgzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmY1ZjNjOS0wMTRhLWVhMTEtYTFjYy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GxOtjlv5EfFjooP7i3irRVE6kWw6tP_R0Z0DRSwl5HQ=&emci=0026f935-e749-ea11-a1cc-00155d03b1e8&emdi=aff5f3c9-014a-ea11-a1cc-00155d03b1e8&ceid=677700)
**What’s the West Virginia Recovery First Plan?**
⚠️ Expanding long-term residential treatment options and vocational training for those struggling with addiction. West Virginia doesn’t have enough beds to properly treat addiction. Effective treatment must include long-term treatment options.
⚠️ Opioid manufacturers must pay to clean up their mess. My plan includes a tax on opioid distributors that will be directed toward drug treatment centers across the state. It’s immoral for opioid manufacturer CEOs to make $10 million bonuses while average West Virginians suffer. They will no longer profit from our pain.
⚠️ Implementing a comprehensive prevention program that will help our children steer clear of drugs from an early age. This means working with schools, law enforcement, and the courts to keep our kids in the classroom and out of the courtroom. I’ll also give local law enforcement the resources to go after drug dealers.
I am ready to bring home the results we desperately need - [**add your name to support comprehensive, long-term solutions to this crisis.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/14758361/155042820/-2062289957?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODgzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmY1ZjNjOS0wMTRhLWVhMTEtYTFjYy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GxOtjlv5EfFjooP7i3irRVE6kWw6tP_R0Z0DRSwl5HQ=&emci=0026f935-e749-ea11-a1cc-00155d03b1e8&emdi=aff5f3c9-014a-ea11-a1cc-00155d03b1e8&ceid=677700)
Together, we will move West Virginia forward.
Ben Salango
Ben Salango for WV
---
Ben Salango for West Virginia
PO Box 11650
Charleston, WV 25339
United States

name: Mary Mancini
subject: TNDP Weekly Recap: Stand with Vindman & Republicans Broken Promises
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
STAND WITH TNDP VETERANS AND
MILITARY FAMILIES CAUCUS
http://bit.ly/tndenblackburn [http://bit.ly/tndenblackburn]
The Tennessee Democratic Party Veterans and Military Families Caucus released an open letter to address Republican Senator Marsha Blackburn’s attack on Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman. Instead of defending the decorated veteran and his sacrifices for our country, Senator Blackburn contributed to the witness backlash by sending a series of tweets questioning his patriotism and ultimately resulting in the loss of Lt. Col. Vindman’s job. The letter denounces Senator Blackburn and asks her to apologize for treating a member of the military in such a disrespectful way simply because she disagreed with him. All Tennesseeans are invited to stand with Veterans and Military Families by signing the letter denouncing Senator Blackburn's behavior. Sign the letter now! [http://bit.ly/tndenblackburn]
REPUBLICANS DEFEND CONFEDERATE
GENERAL AND VOTE TO KEEP
NATHAN BEDFORD FORREST BUST
AT CAPITOL
First things first, when someone pulls out the "politically correct" card it means they're trying to control the debate to ultimately stop social change and progress.
Representative Rick Staples brought legislation again this week to remove the Nathan Bedford Forrest bust from a place of honor in the State Capitol. Republicans continued to argue against the removal. Republican Representative Sexton went as far to say, "There wasn't enough data to know if Nathan Bedford Forrest was a bad person. He made mistakes, and all humans make mistakes.....There's a lot of pressure for people to be politically correct and I don't want to go down that road." Republicans then voted against the legislation and in favor of keeping the bust.
In the words of Democratic Representative Bo Mitchell, “This is not political correctness, this is historical correctness.... We know what this is about and you're either for or against the man who took up arms against the United States.”

clean_body: STAND WITH TNDP VETERANS AND MILITARY FAMILIES CAUCUS [] The Tennessee Democratic Party Veterans and Military Families Caucus released an open letter to address Republican Senator Marsha Blackburn’s attack on Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman. Instead of defending the decorated veteran and his sacrifices for our country, Senator Blackburn contributed to the witness backlash by sending a series of tweets questioning his patriotism and ultimately resulting in the loss of Lt. Col. Vindman’s job. The letter denounces Senator Blackburn and asks her to apologize for treating a member of the military in such a disrespectful way simply because she disagreed with him. All Tennesseeans are invited to stand with Veterans and Military Families by signing the letter denouncing Senator Blackburn's behavior. Sign the letter now! [] REPUBLICANS DEFEND CONFEDERATE GENERAL AND VOTE TO KEEP NATHAN BEDFORD FORREST BUST AT CAPITOL First things first, when someone pulls out the "politically correct" card it means they're trying to control the debate to ultimately stop social change and progress. Representative Rick Staples brought legislation again this week to remove the Nathan Bedford Forrest bust from a place of honor in the State Capitol. Republicans continued to argue against the removal. Republican Representative Sexton went as far to say, "There wasn't enough data to know if Nathan Bedford Forrest was a bad person. He made mistakes, and all humans make mistakes.....There's a lot of pressure for people to be politically correct and I don't want to go down that road." Republicans then voted against the legislation and in favor of keeping the bust. In the words of Democratic Representative Bo Mitchell, “This is not political correctness, this is historical correctness.... We know what this is about and you're either for or against the man who took up arms against the United States.” PRESIDENTIAL PRIMARY EARLY VOTING BEGINS WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 12TH [] Early voting begins this upcoming Wednesday, February 12th and runs through February 25th. A flat tire, sleeping through an alarm, or a meeting that runs late. Life happens, so don't let it stop you from voting. Get ahead and vote early. Find your polling location and make a plan to vote now! [] LISTEN NOW: TNDP PODCAST [] On Monday, we heard Governor Lee's State of the State, which focused heavily on education and possible investments. In our podcast, TNDP Chair, Mary Mancini sits down with Tennessee Education Association President, Beth Brown. Hear first-hand the current state of education. [] HELP PROTECT THE VOTE [] Find out how you can help protect the vote and ensure everyone's voice is heard at the polls this year. These training sessions will teach you the basics of voter protection and how to become a poll watcher on Super Tuesday, March 3rd 2020. Register for an event near you now: Nashville [] Knoxville [] Chattanooga [] Memphis [] BECOME A DNC DELEGATE [] The deadline to apply to be a DNC Delegate is less than 30 days away. Visit TNDP's website for more details. [] We are hosting a series of workshops across the state to explain the process for becoming a delegate. R [] egister for a Delegate Selection Workshop near you! [] TWEETS FROM THE WEEK [] [] INVEST IN DEMOCRACY Disgusted by Marsha Blackburn's behavior ? Discouraged by Governor Lee and the Republican legislator's broken promises? The most impactful thing you can do to make the change you want to see in Tennessee, is to vote. But we also have to have strong candidates running good campaigns if we want to win and that's where your investment in the our work is crucial. It’s expensive to maintain and expand TNDP operations in an election year. Paying for things like poll watcher trainings, candidate recruitment efforts and campaign support are expensive – that’s why every dollar you contribute helps us win elections and fight back. Even though our district elected President Trump by nearly 16%, he’s been working with Nancy Pelosi and Adam Schiff to kick him out of office!

Tonight is the last Democratic debate before the New Hampshire primary. He only hopes one of the candidates on stage will be good enough to beat President Trump in November (spoiler alert: they won’t be).

It wouldn’t surprise me if he supported **Bernie Sanders** or **Elizabeth Warren**. Since he was elected, Anthony Brindisi has shown his true, ultra-liberal colors.

Although he claims to be bipartisan, he only votes with President Trump 9.2% of the time! That’s less than Nancy Pelosi, Adam Schiff, and even self-proclaimed socialist Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez.

Or he might be a faithful supporter of **Joe Biden**. Last January, he threw away his vote for Speaker of the House and voted for Joe Biden (who can’t even be Speaker, anyway). By not supporting an actual Representative, he essentially handed the gavel to Nancy Pelosi.

He might also support New York City liberal **Mike Bloomberg**. After all, Bloomberg did give $10 million to House Majority PAC, a super PAC that supports failing Democrats like Anthony.

body:
---
| | Doug Jones: "Doing right is not a courageous act, it is simply following your oath." \- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - **Alabama Senator Doug Jones faces the toughest reelection race of any Democrat in November -- and he voted to convict President Trump.** [**We need more political courage like this! Sign our card to thank Doug Jones for taking a stand for what 's right, no matter the cost >>**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c1/2066499597/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1+-+We+need+more+political+courage+like+this&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2") [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c0/2066499597/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2") Doug Jones has been serving his country with integrity for decades. Before running for Senate, Doug fought for civil rights as a U.S. Attorney, successfully prosecuting two Ku Klux Klan members for the deadly 1963 Birmingham church bombing. This morning, Senator Jones issued a statement, saying: "On the day I was sworn in as a United States Senator, I took an oath to protect and defend the Constitution. At the beginning of the impeachment trial, I took a second oath to do 'impartial justice' according to the same Constitution I swore to protect... I did not run for Senate hoping to participate in the impeachment trial of a duly-elected President, but **I cannot and will not shrink from my duty to defend the Constitution and to do impartial justice.** ... After many sleepless nights, I have reluctantly concluded that**the evidence is sufficient to convict the President for both abuse of power and obstruction of Congress.** " Pay attention, Senate Republicans -- THIS is what fulfilling the oath of office looks like! [**Today, despite everything else that 's going on, let's take a minute to THANK Senator Jones for putting country over politics >>**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c3/2066499597/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+Today+despite+everything+else+thatrsquos&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2") [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c2/2066499597/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2") All too often, we're reaching out with news of a politician behaving badly. We're grateful for Doug Jones for taking a political risk to do the right thing. **We need more of this!** Thanks for signing on, \-- the Voter Protection Project P.S. Every dollar of our Senate Battleground Fund will be used to **ELECT courageous Democrats like Doug Jones and DEFEAT spineless Trump-enablers** like Susan Collins, Thom Tillis, Joni Ernst, and Mitch McConnell. **[We're almost at our first 1,000 donor goal -- can you chip in today?](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c5/2066499597/VEsD/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vpp_flipthesenate_feb?refcode=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&amount=25&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=5+-+Wersquore+almost+at+our+first+1000+donor&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2")**
---

| [Voter Protection Project](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0599/72412298/4ef20a89/610460c4/2066499597/VEsA/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protectvotingorg?refcode=protectvotingorg_headerlogo&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=6+-+Voter+Protection+Project&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2&source=VPP_EM_EN_200207_DougJones_U2_X2")

name: Christopher Zook Jr.
subject: Calling All Volunteers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

With all the news coming out of Washington this week, it has never been more clear that we need to replace Lizzie Fletcher with a conservative like Wesley Hunt!

But we can't wait until September or October to get our message out. We need to start **NOW**.

Stop by one of our three block-walking locations tomorrow to help us get the message out to your friends, neighbors and family members.

We need your help to get Wesley elected and to bring new leadership to Washington!

To RSVP, just email me back at Chris@wesleyfortexas.com.

Sincerely,

Christopher Zook Jr.
Political Director

****[CONTRIBUTE](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=ea82e39c24cc49e1876b661bf1dcefef&_e=4jzNC-yRR9kovmEOkFYSFhXLIvUj5-58_5QT4fCuhc4N1wvNPRPSJcUNNXy7Pv8C_4XsLmh44hM9sM3IubxwfcmmmRtHXywjWpO4aUuPyCClnBllnEJqJpCkEaBvevmSkhjrMXVhptAzJLQXNda7oK4umNaflivKvMLYaxiWnsYmvRm62NYGjT9U03n_d2m5Aqm_r_75Q0UdcW0x0jkybMLQt8hMiP0uwjWw1N0I_YM81yxyPCIdUpb85nUpUJjCqSfde-f0vQTnsSIdf2o_nnF8Cv-N_D4l-RRc-sdfmrI%3D)****

name: Warren for President
subject: Other candidates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Other candidates are leaning on mega-wealthy donors and Super PACs to boost their campaigns advertising efforts -- and that means right now, we're being outspent in New Hampshire in the critical... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852adfe/45c30650/1fb4644c2/66e26dac/1536736453/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Other candidates are leaning on mega-wealthy donors and Super PACs to boost their campaigns' advertising efforts -- and that means right now, we're being outspent in New Hampshire in the critical final days before the primary.

**[Elizabeth needs your support now more than ever before-- can she count on you to chip in $2 or whatever you can to help fight back and close the gap?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852adfe/45c30650/1fb4644c2/66e26daf/1536736453/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWZlYjIwZnItbWFyY2gzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU8yNEZfRUdMX05GRl9QXzIwMjAwMjA3X1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-feb20fr-march3?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMO24F_EGL_NFF_P_20200207_V1S1_P")**

Earlier this week, we set a goal of $2 million by Tuesday in order to execute our organizing and advertising program in New Hampshire.

Unlike other candidates in the race, Elizabeth won't be spending a single minute on the phone calling wealthy donors to reach this goal. She won't be going to fancy, closed-door fundraisers where you can only talk with her if you write a big enough check. And she won't be leaning on outside PACs.

Instead, Elizabeth will spend this week talking with voters at town halls taking unscripted questions. She'll be meeting people, taking selfies, and reading the notes that supporters pass to her in line. (And if you donate to this email, she might even give you a call!)

**Elizabeth is running her campaign differently not because it's easy, but because it's the right thing to do. But that means we're only going to reach this goal with small-dollar contributions from grassroots supporters like you.**

**[We've made great progress so far, but we still need a boost of support to push our campaign over the top with just four days to go. Can Elizabeth count on you to chip in today and help reach our $2 million goal?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852adfe/45c30650/1fb4644c2/66e26daf/1536736453/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWZlYjIwZnItbWFyY2gzIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU8yNEZfRUdMX05GRl9QXzIwMjAwMjA3X1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-feb20fr-march3?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMO24F_EGL_NFF_P_20200207_V1S1_P")**
| _If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

clean_body: Other candidates are leaning on mega-wealthy donors and Super PACs to boost their campaigns advertising efforts -- and that means right now, we're being outspent in New Hampshire in the critical... | | | "" Other candidates are leaning on mega-wealthy donors and Super PACs to boost their campaigns' advertising efforts -- and that means right now, we're being outspent in New Hampshire in the critical final days before the primary. "" Earlier this week, we set a goal of $2 million by Tuesday in order to execute our organizing and advertising program in New Hampshire. Unlike other candidates in the race, Elizabeth won't be spending a single minute on the phone calling wealthy donors to reach this goal. She won't be going to fancy, closed-door fundraisers where you can only talk with her if you write a big enough check. And she won't be leaning on outside PACs. Instead, Elizabeth will spend this week talking with voters at town halls taking unscripted questions. She'll be meeting people, taking selfies, and reading the notes that supporters pass to her in line. (And if you donate to this email, she might even give you a call!) Elizabeth is running her campaign differently not because it's easy, but because it's the right thing to do. But that means we're only going to reach this goal with small-dollar contributions from grassroots supporters like you. "" | If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: | | | | "" | "" | | "" | "" | | "" | "" Thanks, Team Warren "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2020 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Re: Trump's visit to Colorado
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
Hello -- About a minute from now, I'm going to ask you to add a contribution to our grassroots campaign for Senate as part of an emergency fundraising drive we're organizing this week. Let me explain why this is so important: **Donald Trump announced last night that he 's coming to Colorado to campaign for our opponent Cory Gardner, just days after Gardner stood with Senate Republicans to block firsthand witness testimony and cover-up for the president's abuses of power.** This is the beginning of a new effort to tear down our campaign, and we're expecting Trump to attack me personally and for those attacks to be amplified by Mitch McConnell's dark money donor network. We have to fight back today. Trump and McConnell's attack machine is breaking fundraising records left and right, and the only way we can counter them is by collecting as many grassroots donations as possible. So, we've set an ambitious goal of raising $250,000 by tomorrow night, and we need you to make a contribution of any amount to help us get there: **Can I count on you to make an urgent donation of $10 or more to my campaign right now? I need your support to fight back against whatever Trump and McConnell have planned for our campaign in the coming weeks and months. name: Steve Sisolak
subject: I want to hear from you, team
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Committee to Elect Steve Sisolak
body:
We came into this fight with one goal: bring people together and get work done.
|
---
[ ](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/qdgw9F04OpjjBIpdRgzkIfy7eHe6_BqIednqJssk7v4=)

| | Hey team -- **We came into this fight with one goal: bring people together and get work done.** And together this team has been a shining example of what we can accomplish when we do that: We’ve worked to give teachers a raise, increase the minimum wage, and drive down the cost of health care through the creation of the Patient Protection Commision. **But we have more work ahead to create a Nevada that works for everyone.[I’ve traveled all across Nevada to hear from voters directly, but I want to make sure everyone has a chance to share their thoughts -- Can you share what issues are most important to you in 2020?](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/hwY3rrIc1ktp6Zm9OENhDljsNBVSWeak5Cb2RxAvcok=)** | | | [2020 Survey](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/hwY3rrIc1ktp6Zm9OENhDljsNBVSWeak5Cb2RxAvcok=)
---
Right now, all eyes are on early swing states like Nevada as our nation gears up for one of the most influential presidential elections in decades, and with that will come an unprecedented amount of organizing and spending for Presidential races in our state.
**
But we can’t lose sight of the work that needs to be done in order to grow our state’s economy and rebuild our public education systems.
**
This new year is an opportunity for us to come together and continue making gains for Nevada’s future.**With so much at stake, it’s important for me to get a sense of your biggest concerns as we move forward --**[ Will you take a moment to fill out my team’s quick 2020 survey?](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/hwY3rrIc1ktp6Zm9OENhDljsNBVSWeak5Cb2RxAvcok=) | | [Take the Survey](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/hwY3rrIc1ktp6Zm9OENhDljsNBVSWeak5Cb2RxAvcok=)
---
Thanks for your input,
Steve
| [ ](https://act.myngp.com/el/kMiXCAvhFTAYW28Kk5bB7veUNxXrGkZHE5ne0QnUaXM=/B2LHRMWlc91G8jWgWYbxyQEf_tSi4Zh_x7NLvXYS5RE=)
---

name: James Iacino
subject: February is the month to recognize black history and its significance to our country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James for Colorado
body:
| Black history is American history. Dear friends, February is Black History Month. It’s easy to send one email, write a social media post, and call it a day. There is a checklist for politicians to do the bare minimum and feel they have properly honored the history of black Americans. But that is a gross injustice. The reality is our nation was built on the backs of black bodies and the healing process from the long term damage this caused is far from over. We take the month of February to celebrate the accomplishments black Americans made in the face of oppression. Our shared culture, history, and American exceptionalism would not be possible without the contributions of black Americans. A good elected official works for ALL of their constituents, and that’s what I will do as your Congressman. We still have a lot of work to do before we can reach true equality, and that’s why I’m in this fight. This February, let’s not lose sight of what’s important. With gratitude, James Iacino
---
Paid for by James for Colorado
---
James Iacino for Congress
PO Box 26
Montrose, CO 81401
United States

name: Doug Jones
subject: Why I voted to convict Donald Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
#
Team,
Senator Rosen said I could reach out because Wednesday, I voted to convict Donald Trump on both charges brought against him by Congress, and we could really use your help.
I'm already in the middle of a tough re-election campaign, but that didn't cross my mind when I weighed the evidence in the trial according to my Constitutional oaths.
Just like folks in Nevada are counting on Senator Rosen, I know the people of Alabama are counting on me to do the right thing. My vote isn't going to make Mitch McConnell or Donald Trump happy, though. And they're already coming after me for it. One group spent a half a million dollars last week, and more is on the way.
[I wouldn't ask if I didn't need your help to take on McConnell and fight back. Will you split a donation between my campaign and Jacky Rosen today?](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409df129/4fbb2af3/22e853ea/5e80d930/2410866076/VEsH/p/eyJlM3RoWW5CaFoyVWdmWDA9IjoianItZG91Zy1qb25lcy0yMDIwIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbTIwMDIwN2kiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-doug-jones-2020?refcode=em200207i&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
The evidence clearly proves that Donald Trump coerced a foreign government to interfere in our election for his personal political benefit, placing his personal interest above our national interests. I also believe that the president deliberately obstructed Congress by blocking testimony from witnesses and the production of documents during the course of the investigation. This is unacceptable, but it didn't seem to bother Mitch McConnell.
The stakes are historically high, but to Mitch McConnell and his enablers in Washington, no tactics are off-limits.
That's not how I operate. I will continue working hard in the Senate for what's right -- defending our health care, civil rights, and equality -- but I can't do it alone.
[If you care about keeping courageous voices who will fight for what's right for the country -- not what's right for Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump -- pitch in $5 to support me and Jacky Rosen today.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409df129/4fbb2af3/22e853ea/5e80d930/2410866076/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoWW5CaFoyVWdmWDA9IjoianItZG91Zy1qb25lcy0yMDIwIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbTIwMDIwN2kiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-doug-jones-2020?refcode=em200207i&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
Thank you for your support.
Doug Jones
U.S. Senator, Alabama
**[Contribute](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409df129/4fbb2af3/22e853ea/5e80d930/2410866076/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoWW5CaFoyVWdmWDA9IjoianItZG91Zy1qb25lcy0yMDIwIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbTIwMDIwN2kiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jr-doug-jones-2020?refcode=em200207i&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409df129/4fbb2af3/22e853ea/5e80d931/2410866076/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=Nljwc1Nw52ha-mlzdBXxLUA "https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

name: Team Monika
subject: Less than a month away!! We need volunteers!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MONIKA FOR CONGRESS |
body:
Did you see Monika's email from Friday? We wanted to make sure that you saw her message and have time to act. We are now only $9,860 away from our end of the month goal. Pitch in and help us keep going.

-Team Monika

Friend,

I wish I was emailing you today to talk about my platform, why I am running for Congress, my career experiences, or sharing a proud moment about my family. But I'm not writing about any of those things today. I am writing because my campaign needs your help, now more than ever. We have been staffing up and talking to voters as much as we can, but unfortunately these things cost a lot of money. We are only 38 days away from the pivotal primary Election in North Carolina and we are coming up short of our January goal. To keep this campaign moving we need to raise another $16,000 before January 31st. Is there any way you can chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can so we can keep this campaign moving?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

$5 »

$10 »
$25 »

$50 »
$100 »

Other »
With Appreciation,

Monika Johnson-Hostler

Democratic Candidate for Congress, NC-02

**********

Monika Johnson-Hostler is running for Congress to give the people of North Carolina's 2nd District a representative who will listen, work hard, and stay true to their word, doing what's best for the district. As the District 2 member of the Wake County school board and Executive Director of North Carolina's Coalition Against Sexual Assault, Monika knows how to lead with solutions. For Monika Johnson-Hostler, serving people is about integrity, and that's what she'll bring to Congress.

Monika for Congress
PO BOX 99092
Raleigh, NC 27624

name: Michael Bennet
subject: every day counts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| |
---
| Good afternoon, team,

We've only got a few days until New Hampshire votes. [Make no mistake: This will be the most important moment of the campaign.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbaa9a/134673f2/37d7c7be/69973a74/1446876837/VEsH/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bfa-feb?refcode=EM_FR_B2_020720_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,29,50,100,250,500,1000")

Since the start of this race, we've been counted out time and time again.

The talking heads and pundits have said we didn't stand a chance - but we proved them wrong.

This small and scrappy campaign has outlasted better-funded and better-known candidates -- and more than half of voters remain undecided.

[But unless we can raise $100,000 by Tuesday, we risk not having the resources needed to compete in the final days of this primary.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbaa9a/134673f2/37d7c7be/69973a74/1446876837/VEsE/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bfa-feb?refcode=EM_FR_B2_020720_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,29,50,100,250,500,1000")

name: Colleen Peters
subject: About our first ad
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN Peters for Michigan
body:
When I talk to folks about my husband Gary
| | | [](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79be/2236176360/VEsH/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://petersformichigan.com/")
---
Hi friend,

When I talk to folks about my husband Gary, they're usually surprised to find out that he was a Lt. Commander in the Navy Reserve - and signed up _again_ after 9/11.

**But people who know Gary aren 't surprised. They know that he's always put service before himself - it's just in his DNA. It's why non-partisan groups have ranked him as one of the most effective senators. And it's what drives him every day in Washington when he's fighting for Michigan. **

That's what I love about Gary. He's always thinking about how he can do more for working families, more for our troops and veterans, more for Michiganders. And that's what our first TV ad is all about.
Since you're reading this email, I know you're one of Gary's strongest supporters, friend. That's why I wanted to give you a sneak peek of our first ad of the cycle - it talks all about Gary's record of fighting for our state, and I think you're really gonna like it.

[**You 'll see our ad live if you're watching the Michigan vs. Michigan State game tomorrow, but you can also click here to check it out before anyone else.**](https://secure.petersformichigan.?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email_tv-ad-colleen?refcode=jtk302-fr-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1&amount=3")
[](https://secure.petersformichigan.%3D/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email_tv-ad-colleen?refcode=jtk302-fr-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")
I'm so excited for the rest of Michigan to see what I already know: Gary is one of the hardest-working senators because he truly cares about doing the work it takes for Michigan families.

Thanks for your support - it truly means the world to Gary and me.

\- Colleen [Contribute](https://secure.petersformichigan.%3D/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email_tv-ad-colleen?refcode=jtk302-fr-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")

| [To unsubscribe from the Peters for Michigan email list, please click here.](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79bb/2236176360/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN Peters for Michigan
Po Box 32072
Detroit, MI 48244 Senator Gary Peters stands up for middle-class families in Michigan and across America every day. He is fighting for quality education, increasing job growth and protecting our values. Gary is working hard to stand up to the extreme Trump agenda and turn the Senate blue, and the path to taking back Congress runs right through Michigan. Join Gary's team and show your support [here.](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79ba/2236176360/VEsA/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/peters_email?refcode=email-footer") This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com | Change or update your email address [here.](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79b9/2236176360/VEsB/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/s/update-your-info") We believe that emails are a crucial way to stay in touch with our top supporters. However, if you'd still like to receive fewer emails, you can click [here](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79b8/2236176360/VEsO/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails"). If you would like to unsubscribe from our emails, click [here.](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79bb/2236176360/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") [Donate to Gary ->](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da9f9/5721a3ed/70f1e65e/efb79ba/2236176360/VEsHBQ/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/peters_email?refcode=email-footer") |

name: Genevieve Collins
subject: This race has entered its final month!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Collins for Texas, Inc
body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001k7wcfyWlsq0Eg0ZM0I7dzT1SsfnO-MMsON2DENDgy3ek8CUtzlEKU85T0rlzJIMc_4RwnJIU8luNiqmtn9wvYjwE4FNENxnRl9jOCpayc53D8I-tRGh8qRtkjskmO-2V49a8yJpe6r0ivZkzvNtDr7VETKxcPqwDSX78_KAWQfakBq4pXjjWNERr-vStw_8Z&c=2XsC5PMSYBpmdBp_UWGlOLE3fhI_1uLnCjDkrdfsC7VMlIUwjBhQZA==&ch=6PurKPW5FtDSVASRjI8WgWmUaj-zrs_SAEtLZPqNct_R3IaDKY7omA==)
---
| Peter,
We're officially on the home stretch of the campaign, and that means we're busier than ever crisscrossing the 32nd District and meeting voters.
---
| Our campaign was everywhere in the district this week, attending multiple events every single day.
From supporting our Texas Game Wardens at the Stewards of The Wild dinner, to celebrating the Lunar New Year, speaking to the SMU College Republicans, and watching the State of the Union with the Dallas County Republican Party, we definitely kept busy!
It's so fun to go to these events across the district, and the response we receive is incredible. We can definitely feel the momentum and support for the campaign growing each and every day.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001k7wcfyWlsq0Eg0ZM0I7dzT1SsfnO-MMsON2DENDgy3ek8CUtzlEKUyuIfHXYwuA5KxGboYGO41DLKvLb4Z9avkYBumQZwEqxTk-3vOFXLEngWc9zmwtO4kmsoqAv5AMKosA8k6yLIrE-WAIGYGpEeJcVwPwWlyvfavOZR_9XF4-RY2hovDqr38DV_esB1WwbT1SPnlIbcYc=&c=2XsC5PMSYBpmdBp_UWGlOLE3fhI_1uLnCjDkrdfsC7VMlIUwjBhQZA==&ch=6PurKPW5FtDSVASRjI8WgWmUaj-zrs_SAEtLZPqNct_R3IaDKY7omA==)
---
| The endorsements also keep coming in from both current and former elected officials. This week, our campaign was endorsed by Congressman Lance Gooden and former State Sen. John Carona. I appreciate their support in this fight!
---
|
---
|
---
| Finally, we have our Team Genevieve Day of Action tomorrow morning. We will be block walking and phone banking throughout the day. These events are critical to our victory, so we need all the volunteers we can get. Click/tap below to get more info and RSVP to our Facebook event. I hope we'll see you there!
---
| Volunteer Opportunity!
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001k7wcfyWlsq0Eg0ZM0I7dzT1SsfnO-MMsON2DENDgy3ek8CUtzlEKUyuIfHXYwuA5B_DrVvFEDvh87hdJMsiqDN4BcWH_c4Rtpw62GnzYggaphQfiTXF2iQhESGbwSXgkNN0vImxtNPrE5DNgCcZA2VExhXuuLR_BXKkI85OUjqUDEvhj9Dk3gw==&c=2XsC5PMSYBpmdBp_UWGlOLE3fhI_1uLnCjDkrdfsC7VMlIUwjBhQZA==&ch=6PurKPW5FtDSVASRjI8WgWmUaj-zrs_SAEtLZPqNct_R3IaDKY7omA==)
---
| Now that our campaign has entered its final month, we're busier than ever and there's so much to update you all on every week! I hope I'll see you tomorrow and at future events or volunteer opportunities. Thank you in advance to everyone who turns out tomorrow!
---
|
---
| Genevieve
---
| |

---
It’s debate night, and we’re excited to watch Tom! Are you ready to watch? The Democratic debate in Manchester, New Hampshire will kick off at 8:00 p.m. Eastern/5:00 p.m. Pacific Time. **Remember, if you just text the word****DEBATE****to the phone number 46-866 right now, we’ll send you a quick heads-up before it starts so you don’t miss a minute!** Oh, and don’t worry — we’re not going to bother you with tons of messages. We’ll only send you a debate reminder and the occasional critical update, but you can always change your messaging preferences directly via text, or reach out to us by replying here to _[info@tomsteyer.com](mailto:info@tomsteyer.com)_. Counting down to the big event,
Tom 2020

name: SaraGideon.com
subject: This is the excuse Senator Collins gave for why she voted to acquit President Trump -- and Trump's response a few hours later.
body:
"I believe the president has learned..."

Here's the excuse Susan Collins gave for why she voted to acquit President Trump.
[](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802932/4bef7a87/6d589647/287fbc28/2396136701/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMDctMi1hIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gideon2020?refcode=em200207-2-a")
And here's Trump's response a few hours later.
[](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802932/4bef7a87/6d589647/287fbc28/2396136701/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMDctMi1hIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gideon2020?refcode=em200207-2-a")
The idea that Donald Trump has learned any kind of lesson from impeachment is absurd. We're sick of Senator Collins' excuses for standing with Trump and McConnell -- are you?
**[Add your $3 contribution now to defeat Susan Collins and finally take back the Senate for Democrats-> ](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802932/4bef7a87/6d589647/287fbc28/2396136701/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMDctMi1hIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gideon2020?refcode=em200207-2-a")**
Look, nothing will change in Washington until we kick Mitch McConnell out as Majority Leader -- and that means we have to win in Maine.
Keep fighting,
Team Sara
**********[CONTRIBUTE](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802932/4bef7a87/6d589647/287fbc28/2396136701/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAyMDctMi1hIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gideon2020?refcode=em200207-2-a")**********

* * *
Sara Gideon for Maine
PO Box 812
South Freeport, ME 04078

name: Lisa Scheller
subject: come and take them
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LISA SCHELLER FOR CONGRESS [Unsubscribe](http://links
body:
Just like we thought they would, the Gun Control Lobby is coming for our firearms. Peter, Just like we thought they would, the Gun Control Lobby is coming for our firearms. Peter, the Giffords Gun Center – one of the biggest gun grabbing groups in the COUNTRY – has just endorsed Susan Wild for Congress.
Why do they like Susan? Because she supports their radical agenda to strip down our 2nd Amendment rights.
Peter, what do we say to the radicals who want to take away our Constitutional rights? [ **COME AND TAKE THEM** ](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=34c895c20d6347298cce69144cd8a3e8&_e=tgKrxNQ2WfRDfLFJXGi2wa3nYVliptgaKLpXXLKqeRw_gvXtwByCoDBvh2vacASKNwyrhYwA5MYRvupxmacbidd3uEXTmfSl7bHRotLIvfR14NkTR61CO2TulvjHzy-3KlqMhfgym5QdVCpKHk-dfNmVJXgpCQ5wU-m3J_MAyZgDGGkkNgM_1AJHgt75sMmKxzXBaszJ83uyJuaCSFQR2X6ACeZEGWHN44Fg1D8reRrGBuOFAOeJ5-YuT-ZbZHP04FkPmHcJ9Q6YpwBycKhSzetYgif-ka0c8yazP0g-2TuSPEGae2Kh9pmBt0O_cIOQTdd63JqjEq3phZrxlKNYw95VomnDL4reu2Q-28NwVSEl-jG6TkZWwG9HN4tb_1sZ0Li3K8-6EPAWZEuyPAhLy9A3anE7_HSVz012i8WuzwJowOyADgfpeGrW_ejd5Jbr) We’re not going to just give up our right to defend our homes, our families, and our values. Friend: GOP congressman Chip Roy wants NOTHING more than to take away affordable health care for millions of Americans. He can't wait for the Affordable Care Act to get dismantled through a pending federal lawsuit - in fact, he just led a conservative think-tank panel about this very topic!

subject: In honor of today's anniversary:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Persist has become a rallying cry. Because that night three years ago wasn't about Elizabeth. It was about every single person who was tired of being told to sit down and shut up. It's a powerful... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852adf4/45c30650/1fb2ba452/66e21538/3871591723/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Three years ago today, Mitch McConnell tried to silence Elizabeth Warren -- and silence the words of Coretta Scott King -- on the floor of the U.S. Senate.

"Senator Warren was giving a lengthy speech. She had appeared to violate the rule. She was warned. She was given an explanation. **Nevertheless, she persisted** ," he said.

Since then, "persist" has become a rallying cry. Because that night three years ago wasn't about Elizabeth. It was about every single person who was tired of being told to sit down and shut up.

And today, it's a powerful reminder that change doesn't happen all at once -- it takes every single one of us, fighting hard every day for what we believe in.

**Because if we don't fight, we can't win -- and when we persist together, big, structural change can happen.**

**[Today we celebrate all the members of our movement who persist. Click here to get your "persist" shirts, mugs, buttons, and more today to support this grassroots campaign.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852adf4/45c30650/1fb2ba452/66e2153b/3871591723/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU8zOEZfTUVSX1NSWV9aXzIwMjAwMjA3X1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://shop.elizabethwarren.com/collections/nevertheless-she-persisted?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326&utm_campaign=wfp&utm_medium=email&utm_source=EMO38F_MER_SRY_Z_20200207_V1S3_P")**

It's a powerful... | | | "" Three years ago today, Mitch McConnell tried to silence Elizabeth Warren -- and silence the words of Coretta Scott King -- on the floor of the U.S. Senate. "Senator Warren was giving a lengthy speech. She had appeared to violate the rule. She was warned. She was given an explanation. Nevertheless, she persisted ," he said. Since then, "persist" has become a rallying cry. Because that night three years ago wasn't about Elizabeth. It was about every single person who was tired of being told to sit down and shut up. And today, it's a powerful reminder that change doesn't happen all at once -- it takes every single one of us, fighting hard every day for what we believe in. Because if we don't fight, we can't win -- and when we persist together, big, structural change can happen. "" "" | "" Keep persisting, Team Warren Elizabeth doesn't accept contributions from PACs of any kind or federally registered lobbyists. This grassroots movement is powered by supporters like you. name: Susan
subject: FWD: My vote to acquit President Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Collins for Senator
body:
It's been a whirlwind week, and I wanted to make sure you saw the email I sent on Wednesday. I hope you will take a moment to read my note below about my vote to acquit President Trump.
To unsubscribe click [here](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=NDE3MzMzOTAS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDYyOTc2NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)

[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=NDE3MzMzOTAS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDYyOTc2NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)
_****_

Peter,
It's been a whirlwind week, and I wanted to make sure you saw the email I sent on Wednesday.
I hope you will take a moment to read my note below about my vote to acquit President Trump.
Thank you for standing by my side.
All my best,
Susan

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Susan Collins
Date: Wednesday, February 5, 2020
Subject: My vote to acquit President Trump
To: < dpwillis67@gmail.com >

Moments ago, I voted to acquit President Trump.
The criticism from my political opponents has been swift and harsh. Part of that might just be that they are mad that I refused to give into their attempts to pressure me. I also believe that the partisan origins of this proceeding -- impeachment passed the House without a single Republican vote -- was an indication of some of the weaknesses of their case.
My opponents also don't seem to share my faith in the voters when it comes to deciding who our elected officials should be. It was important for me to take my oath to do impartial justice seriously. I listened closely, I took extensive notes, and I asked questions of both sides. Ultimately, I concluded that the House Managers failed to prove their case. Here is a brief excerpt from the conclusion of my speech:
_It has been 230 years since George Washington first took the oath of office, and there are good reasons why during that entire time the Senate has never removed a President. Such a move would not only affect the sitting President but could have unpredictable and potentially adverse consequences for public confidence in our electoral process._
_It is my view that, except when extraordinary circumstances require a different result, we should entrust to the people the most fundamental decision of a democracy, namely, who should lead their country._
Peter, I am so grateful to have your support and friendship during this difficult process. **Now that this is over, it is time for the Congress to get back to work on the problems that are facing our great country.**
Thank you for letting me share my views.
Sincerely,
[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=NDE3MzMzOTAS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTcwMDYyOTc2NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)
Senator Susan Collins

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: President Trump keeps winning
body:
After this week, in the face of Nancy Pelosi's attempt to shred our country's progress, President Trump is winning -- and so is America.

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/president-trump-keeps-winning?e=1acd9bb3d6)

President Trump Keeps Winning

After this week, in the face of Nancy Pelosi's attempt to tear apart our country's progress, it's clear that President Doand Trump is winning -- and so is America.

You've seen it play out on the national stage. Last weekend, President Trump took to the airwaves during the Super Bowl to highlight his work reuniting thousands of families through criminal justice reform. He told the story of Alice Marie Johnson, and if you missed it, you should definitely watch it here (https://secure.winred.com/indiana-republican-party/superbowlad?mc_cid=af3d5d4da0&mc_eid=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=4bc66fc124&mc_eid=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

Then, after the Democrats' embarrassing night in Iowa (maybe we'll find out who won their caucus...someday?), President Trump delivered his third State of the Union address, where he talked about America's booming economy, including a record low unemployment rate for women, African Americans and Hispanics. It's a unifying message of the American Comeback, and perhaps his best speech ever. You can watch the full speech here (https://www.whitehouse.gov/sotu/?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

And then on Wednesday, Democrats' five-month abuse of power was finally over, as the Senate voted to acquit President Trump of this sham impeachment. They tried to divide us...but thankfully, President Trump will continue to deliver for Americans.

And this week, Indiana also did its part to make sure this progress continues for another four years. In case you missed it, Second Lady Karen Pence was in Indiana to officially file to place President Trump on your ballot. Thanks to your help, we collected more than 10,000 ballot petition signatures for President Trump -- proof that Hoosiers are organized, motivated, and energized to make sure Indiana is once again first on the board for the Trump-Pence ticket on Election Night!

It's been quite a week, and with your continued help, I know we'll keep this momentum rolling!

-Kyle Hupfer

PHOTO: Second Lady Karen Pence takes a photo with Indiana Governor Eric Holcomb, Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch, Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer, Indiana's National Committee members John Hammond and Anne Hathaway, and more than 60 Trump-Pence supporters.
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was

Bringing the World to Indiana, and Taking Indiana to the World!

Earlier this week, Gov. Holcomb welcomed hundreds of airline decision makers from around the world to Indiana for the Routes Americas 2020 conference! Leaders from roughly 80 airlines and 300 airports were here – giving us an opportunity to showcase our world-class airport and continue to build relationships that will help take Indiana to the world and bring the world to Indiana!

During the conference, Tennessee-based Countour Airlines announced plans to create an aircraft base at Indianapolis International Airport. The airline plans to begin its Indy operations in June with three new routes to Nashville, St. Louis and Pittsburgh – creating up to 55 new jobs by the end of 2023! This is a huge win for Hoosiers that have been demanding non-stop flights to drive-market destinations for business and for leisure. Read more about it here (https://www.insideindianabusiness.com/story/41662738/contour-airlines-coming-to-indy-airport?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

Connecting Hoosiers to better opportunities nationally and internationally is another way that Gov. Holcomb is working to continue Putting People First!

Congress of Counties Recap
Congress of Counties 2020 was a success! Last weekend we hosted more than 400 registered attendees from across the state of Indiana. That's 400 Republicans coming together to help win elections and to keep Indiana on the right track. We hosted tons of breakout sessions on several different campaign topics such as social media, fundraising, technology, and more!

Governor Holcomb (and First Dog Henry Holcomb) also joined in! Governor Holcomb attended Friday for the reception and Saturday for lunch to meet fellow Hoosier Republicans and express his gratitude for the hard work Republicans across the state put in.

Thanks to everyone who joined us and made Congress of Counties a success!

Black History Month

Governor Holcomb has officially declared February as Black History Month! Join us in celebrating and recognizing the achievements of African Americans throughout our nation’s history.
http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Come Join Us at the Republican State Convention!

Filing to be a delegate to the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention is now closed. For those of you not in contested races (or those who are appointed by your county chair), you can now pay your filing fees here (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) . Join us June 19-20 at the Indiana Convention Center to celebrate the results our party's leaders continue to deliver for Hoosiers throughout the state.

--> (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) P (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) ay your delegate fees for the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) <-- (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

Also, today the room blocks for state convention also opened. If you plan to stay overnight in Indianapolis, you can book your room now! Get more information here (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=b8ce59f2e8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

Democrat Disaster in Iowa

On Monday, we saw a total breakdown of the Democrat Caucus in Iowa.

Four years in the making, the Democrats—who want to control your healthcare—couldn’t do something as simple as count votes. We still don’t know the official winner.

Compare that to the Republican Caucus where enthusiasm for President Trump broke records. Never in the history of that event have more people turned out to support an incumbent president. Iowa Democrats were forced to choose between competing shades of socialism, and the night ended as a complete failure and debacle for them. It’s a real metaphor for what happens when Democrats are asked to run things.

If they can’t run their own caucus, how can they run our country?

clean_body: After this week, in the face of Nancy Pelosi's attempt to shred our country's progress, President Trump is winning -- and so is America.

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: I voted to convict Trump. So did this brave man:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864bae/2512058922/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | It has not been an inspiring week in American politics. Instead of standing up for the constitution, the rule of law, and the integrity of our elections, nearly all Republicans in the Senate bowed to a corrupt bully. And since then, we have seen all of Donald Trump's worst qualities on display. **But if you want some inspiration, take a look at U.S. Senator Doug Jones.** The safe move for a guy running for re-election in Alabama would have been to side with Trump, and vote to acquit him. But that's not who Doug Jones is. Remember, he was the prosecutor who doggedly pursued justice against the KKK a quarter century after a church bombing killed four young girls. When it came to Trump, Doug considered the evidence. He listened to his heart. And he took a stand for a better, more accountable America by voting to convict Donald Trump of abuse of power and obstruction of Congress. **To say he 's facing an uphill climb in his re-election campaign in Alabama is putting it lightly.** Which is why he needs our support right now. Doug said that his vote was not an act of political courage, that it was "simply a matter of right and wrong, where doing right is not a courageous act; it is simply following your oath." But we know the truth -- doing right will come at a cost. If you know one thing about Donald Trump and his Twitter troll army, it's that they're viciously vindictive. [**That's why I want us to stand together and support Doug today. Let's keep him in the U.S. Senate.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864baf/2512058922/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?refcode=2020-02-07-jones&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") **2020 is a critical year. Democrats have a lot of seats to pick up, and one big one we must defend.** [**Alabama's a tough place for a Democrat, but Senator Doug Jones held his ground and stood up for what's right. Trump and his deep-pocketed allies will certainly try to punish him, so let's stand with Doug now.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864baf/2512058922/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?refcode=2020-02-07-jones&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") _Jeff_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Jeff Merkley and Doug Jones instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864bac/2512058922/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2020-02-07-jones&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864bad/2512058922/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2020-02-07-jones&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864baa/2512058922/VEsA/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2020-02-07-jones&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2f487/6cd27893/5304eaaf/15864bab/2512058922/VEsB/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/merkley-jones?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2020-02-07-jones&refcode2=express-buttons")
